title: Authenticated Crawls, Sessions, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Compiler Case: Session Isolation
summary: Credentials are copied into configs, sessions cross origins and users, logout links execute, sensitive pages enter shared caches, and retained reports leak personal data. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts session isolation increment in least-authority session and privacy policy. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Partition cookies, storage, caches, checkpoints, reports, and browser contexts by owner, tenant, account, purpose, and run.
Permit safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms and logout or destructive routes, and test same-site side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Partition cookies, storage, caches, checkpoints, reports, and browser contexts by owner, tenant, account, purpose, and run.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Session Isolation model · Theory Session Isolati…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9059 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove least-authority sessions, secrets, safe methods, isolation, minimization, and derived-data deletion; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Partition cookies, storage, caches, checkpoints, reports, and browser contexts by owner, tenant, account, purpose, and run.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Session Isolation model · Theory Session Isolati…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Different targets can share one authenticated browser context for efficiency.
